evidence: Product Hunt launched its Games vertical in June 2015, years before this July 2019 podcast discussion.
Product Hunt launched a dedicated Books vertical in July 2015 (Forbes/Fortune coverage), well before the 2019 pitch.
Product Hunt launched a Podcasts discovery vertical in 2015 per Fast Company, predating this 2019 episode.
Weekend Fund, founded in 2017 by Ryan Hoover and Vedika Jain writing early-stage checks, already existed by the 2019 podcast date.
No evidence this childhood anecdote (crushing itchy seed pods for 25 cents) ever became a real business.
Elance and Upwork (merged 2015) already existed as freelance marketplaces for design/Flash/3D work well before 2019.

No specific company found matching a 'mobile app store with video previews' pitch from this episode; app stores like Apple's already show preview videos but no distinct startup was traced to this idea.
Vungle, a video ad network for mobile games replacing banner ads, was founded in 2011 and was already an established player by 2019.
Shyp, the on-demand shipping startup, operated 2013-2018 and had already shut down (TechCrunch, March 2018) before this July 2019 episode discussed it.
This is general career advice ('become a power user on emerging platforms'), not a specific business/product that could be tracked as built.
